title: dodonaea procumbens
intro: Dodonaea procumbens, also known as Hop Bush, is a small shrub native to Australia that belongs to the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Dodonaea procumbens include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its herbal remedies, and can also be used as a topical cream or ointment for skin conditions.
